facility_name: CyrusOne CHI1 — CME Group Exchange Data Center

notes: KEY FINANCIAL INFRASTRUCTURE. Cooling failure on Nov 27 2025 disrupted global markets. Backup cooling added post-incident. 16-acre campus. Dual 138kV transmission feeds. 54-hour generator run time. Strong retrofit opportunity — current air-cooled chiller system is vulnerable to cascading failures as demonstrated.

facility_name: Equinix NY5 — Financial Exchange Hub (ZutaCore Liquid Cooling)

Part of Equinix's 100+ data center liquid cooling rollout across 45+ metros. Financial trading workloads demand ultra-low-latency, high-density compute. ZutaCore HyperCool uses dielectric fluid that boils at 92F to remove CPU heat.

notes: ENTERPRISE AI CUSTOM SILICON. Dojo D1 chips — 25-chip 5x5 Training Tiles, water-cooled. >200kW per cabinet. Seven ExaPods planned at Palo Alto. Custom CDU. Bare-die contact cold plates with microchannel coolant keeping junction temps <75C.

notes: ENTERPRISE ENERGY SECTOR IMMERSION COOLING. Shell proprietary immersion cooling fluids. GRC single-phase immersion tanks. 100kW/rack. 165,888 AMD EPYC cores. DCD Award finalist. Shell also has immersion cooling at Amsterdam DC (Asperitas partnership).

cooling_type: LiquidStack high-density liquid, direct-to-chip and immersion cooling. Central plant-to-chip integrated thermal management. Engineered for generative AI and hyperscale. Manufacturing + R&D in Texas and Hong Kong.

Part of Q1 2026 liquid cooling M&A wave (Eaton+Boyd $9.5B, Trane+LiquidStack, Samsung+FlaktGroup EUR 1.5B).

facility_name: GRC ICEtank C3 One — US Air Force Hill AFB (Immersion Cooled)
operator_company: US Air Force / GRC (Green Revolution Cooling)
cooling_type: Single-phase liquid immersion cooling — GRC ICEtank containerized system with cooling tower heat rejection. ElectroSafe dielectric coolant. 2-4 purpose-built racks per container. 93.1% reduction in cooling energy. 18.1% less server power. 41.4% total power reduction.
opportunity_summary: US Air Force deployed GRC ICEtank immersion-cooled containerized data centers at Hill AFB. C3 One variant uses cooling tower for heat rejection. Tested cumulatively for 3+ years with 100% uptime. PUE of 1.05. 93.1% reduction in cooling PUE. Turnkey system deployable in under 24 hours. GRC working with USAF on next-gen production units. Strong proof-of-concept for military immersion cooling at scale.
notes: VERIFIED MILITARY IMMERSION COOLING DEPLOYMENT. C3 One at Hill AFB is one of the first confirmed US military immersion-cooled data center deployments. GRC backed by NSF and DoD. Next-gen production units in development with USAF.

facility_name: GRC ICEtank C3 Two — US Air Force Tinker AFB (Immersion Cooled)
operator_company: US Air Force / GRC (Green Revolution Cooling)
cooling_type: Single-phase liquid immersion cooling — GRC ICEtank containerized system with dry cooler heat rejection. ElectroSafe dielectric coolant. 2-4 purpose-built racks per container. 93.1% reduction in cooling energy.
opportunity_summary: US Air Force deployed GRC ICEtank immersion-cooled containerized data centers at Tinker AFB. C3 Two variant uses dry cooler for heat rejection. Tested cumulatively for 3+ years with 100% uptime. PUE of 1.05. Deployable in under 24 hours in wide range of environments. Remote monitoring and control capable.
notes: VERIFIED MILITARY IMMERSION COOLING DEPLOYMENT. C3 Two at Tinker AFB. Dry cooler variant suitable for Oklahoma climate. USAF flagship immersion cooling deployment alongside Hill AFB.

facility_name: NSA Laboratory for Physical Sciences — GRC Immersion Cooling (ICEraQ)
facility_type: Intelligence Agency / Research Lab Data Center
cooling_type: Single-phase liquid immersion cooling — GRC ICEraQ system. ElectroSafe dielectric coolant. Up to 100kW per rack. Eliminates chillers, CRAC units, raised flooring, temperature/humidity controls. 95% cooling energy cost savings. Up to 60% CapEx reduction vs air-cooled.
opportunity_summary: NSA's Advanced Computing Research team at the Laboratory for Physical Sciences has been a GRC immersion cooling customer since 2012. Achieved PUE of 1.05. 100kW per rack (vs 10-15kW with air). 95% cooling energy cost savings. NSA engineer David Prucnal confirmed substantial CapEx reduction. NSA stated potential to cut future data center construction costs in half. Rapid global deployment capability highlighted.
notes: INTELLIGENCE AGENCY IMMERSION COOLING. One of earliest known US government immersion cooling deployments. NSA chose immersion to increase rack density and reduce costs. Exact MW and rack count classified/undisclosed.

facility_name: US Naval Research Laboratory — Two-Phase Immersion Cooling Demo (3M/SGI)
cooling_type: Two-phase liquid immersion cooling — 3M Novec 649 Engineered Fluid. SGI IceX hardware (half-rack, 144 Sandybridge sockets, 40kW+). Dry cooler outside HPC center. Boiling on component surfaces for heat removal. ESTCP-funded demo met energy efficiency and GHG reduction objectives. However, IT equipment failures and costs noted as obstacles to commercialization.
opportunity_summary: NRL hosted ESTCP-funded two-phase immersion cooling demonstration with 3M Novec 649 and SGI IceX hardware. Met DoD energy efficiency and greenhouse gas reduction objectives. Partners: Lawrence Berkeley National Lab, APC/Schneider Electric. Obstacles identified: IT equipment failures in two-phase fluid. Root cause analysis recommended. Important early DoD immersion cooling research site.
notes: DOD RESEARCH DEMO. Important early two-phase immersion cooling test in military context. ESTCP is DoD's environmental technology program. Results informed subsequent DoD cooling decisions. Two-phase viability issues may drive preference toward single-phase (GRC-style) solutions.

opportunity_summary: DISA DECC Mechanicsburg has end-of-life cooling infrastructure (CRAH units). Replacement contract posted Aug 2024. Separately, DISA awarded $931M DCI modernization OTA to HPE (Nov 2025) for hybrid multi-cloud data center upgrades. Strong retrofit candidate as DISA modernizes aging defense computing infrastructure. One of 6+ active DECC locations.
notes: ACTIVE MODERNIZATION TARGET. End-of-life cooling being replaced. $931M HPE modernization contract signals broader DISA DC upgrades. Other DECC sites (Columbus OH, Ogden UT, Oklahoma City, St. Louis) likely face similar aging infrastructure.

facility_name: Sandia National Laboratories — Submer Immersion Cooling HPC Test
facility_type: National Lab / HPC Center (Nuclear Weapons Complex)
cooling_type: Single-phase liquid immersion cooling — Submer Technologies (Barcelona). Biodegradable, nontoxic, nonflammable, noncorrosive dielectric liquid. Whole racks submerged. Viscosity similar to cooking oil. 95% more efficient than traditional cooling. 1,400x better thermal conductor than air. Estimated 70% power reduction for compute-intensive HPC-AI clusters.
opportunity_summary: Sandia National Laboratories formally tested Submer immersion cooling in summer 2024 at its HPC center. Partnership through Adacen Inc. (Albuquerque). opportunity_summary: DUG Technology, Australia's leading geoscience HPC provider, leased 15MW at Skybox Datacenters in Houston's Energy Corridor. 40,000 servers fully immersed in DUG Cool dielectric fluid across 720+ enclosures, delivering 250 petaflops. DUG Cool is a patented immersion system refined over a decade, reducing power consumption by up to 51% vs air cooling by eliminating refrigerated cooling and fans. Serves global oil & gas industry for seismic data processing. Also the largest commercial user of Intel Xeon Phi co-processors (historically). Previously used GRC CarnotJet System at Perth facility.
notes: ENERGY SECTOR (Oil & Gas services). DUG is an Australian geoscience HPC provider serving global oil & gas for seismic exploration. Previously used GRC at Perth. Now operates own DUG Cool immersion system. Also planning 6MW carbon-free DC in Geraldton, Western Australia with potential expansion to multi-exaflop scale (10 data halls). DUG Cool available as containerized 1MW units (10ft, 20ft, 40ft).
